与世界上其他大部分地区一样，制造业有些措手不及。安克公司创始人兼首席执行官史蒂文·杨表示。
“没有人有太大的反应，”杨说，他的电子公司总部设在深圳。“我想这一切都让我们大吃一惊。在我们的中国办事处，每个人都准备去度假过春节。我想第一反应是假期在第一周延长，然后又延长了几天。
人们刚刚下班。他们什么时候可以回来工作还没有一个确定的日期。那段时间是最令人担忧的，因为我们没有展望。他们必须找到确定性。人们不得不在家工作，接触补给等等。头三到四周是最混乱的。“。
2020年初的数据当然反映了伴随而来的制造业放缓。2月份，采购经理人指数(PMI)-用于衡量制造业和服务业健康状况的指标-跌至创纪录低点。
这些瓶颈导致了产品短缺-随着对非必需品的需求下降，许多小企业倒闭，与新冠肺炎相关的裁员开始，这一事实在一些行业变得相对没有意义。仅在4月份，美国就失去了2,050万个工作岗位，失业率达到创纪录的14.7%。(当你突然发现自己无限期失业时，智能手机升级似乎就没那么紧迫了。)。这些事件只会加剧现有的移动趋势，并推迟了5G和其他技术的采用。
新冠肺炎似乎也有可能加速制造业的其他趋势-特别是向制造地点多样化的转变。中国仍然是电子制造业的全球主导力量，但劳动力的代价和政治的不确定性导致许多公司开始将目光投向世界上最大的劳动力之外。
note_en:
The initial impact to China’s electronics industry arrived around the time the nation was celebrating its new year. Two weeks after announcing 59 known cases of a new form of coronavirus, the national government put Wuhan — a city of 11 million — under strict lockdown.
 As with most of the rest of the word, the manufacturing sector was caught somewhat flat-footed. according to  Anker   founder and CEO  Steven Yang  .
 “Nobody had a great reaction,” said Yang, whose electronics company is based in Shenzhen. “I think this all caught us by surprise. In our China office, everybody was prepared to go on vacation for the Chinese New Year. I think the first reaction was that vacation was prolonged the first week and then another several days.
 People were just off work. There wasn’t a determined date for when they could come back to work. That period was the most concerning because we didn’t have an outlook. They had to find certainties. People had to work from home and contact supplies and so forth. That first three to four weeks was the most chaotic.”
 Numbers from early 2020 certainly reflect the  accompanying slowdown in the manufacturing sector. In February, the Purchasing Manager’s Index (PMI) — a metric used to gauge the health of manufacturing and service sectors — hit a record low.
 These bottlenecks resulted in product shortages — a fact that was rendered relatively moot in some sectors as demand for nonessentials dropped, many small businesses shuttered and COVID-19-related layoffs began. The U.S. lost 20.5 million jobs in April alone, hitting a record high  14.7% unemployment. (When you suddenly find yourself indefinitely unemployed, a smartphone upgrade seems much less pressing.) Such events only served to compound existing mobile trends and has delayed the adoption of 5G and other technologies.
 It seems likely, too, that COVID-19 will accelerate other trends within manufacturing — notably, the shift toward diversifying manufacturing sites. China continues to be the dominant global force in electronics manufacturing, but the price of labor and political uncertainty has led many companies to begin looking beyond the world’s largest workforce.
title: 尽管经济放缓，但大流行加速了硬件制造的转移
title_en: Despite slowdowns, pandemic accelerates shifts in hardware manufacturing

note:
这款应用于2014年首次亮相，在苹果经典的Today View小工具提供的功能方面，它是为数不多的突破极限的应用程序之一。在那之后的几年里，这款应用程序充当了常见任务的发射台-比如给最喜欢的联系人发短信，打电话回家，问路，播放你最喜欢的音乐，等等。
现在，您可以在主屏幕上执行相同的操作，但体验更加个性化，符合您当前的iOS14主题。
这款应用程序曾被App Store禁止几个月，因为它存在的唯一原因是作为Today View小部件提供商，而不提供小部件配置以外的其他功能。苹果最终认为Launcher是有价值的，尽管有这个限制，并允许它重返市场。
更重要的是，苹果后来意识到工作流自动化是有市场的，并最终收购了一个类似于启动程序的竞争对手Workflow。Workflow后来变成了苹果的快捷方式应用，并扩展了附加功能-比如集成了Siri。
现在，随着iOS14的发布，苹果已经完全接受了主屏幕可定制小部件的想法。与此同时，用户正在利用快捷方式创建自定义图标，然后使用小工具、图标和壁纸的自定义组合来构建创造性的主屏幕主题。
然而，Launcher为那些不想花几个小时创造定制体验的人提供了一个更简单的选择。
取而代之的是，你可以创建一个包含喜爱的应用程序和任务的小工具，更改小工具的背景颜色，并一次性调整图标的大小。
例如，您现在可以创建启动器小部件，这些小部件允许您轻触图标立即呼叫、发送消息、FaceTime或向收藏的联系人发送电子邮件；获取位置指示；开始在Apple Music中播放艺术家、专辑或播放列表；访问收藏的网站；在应用程序中启动操作(如撰写推文或运行快捷方式)；打开或关闭常用手机设置(如WiFi、蓝牙、低功耗模式、免打扰、飞机模式等)；或者启动设备上的任何其他应用程序。
Launcher的目标是利用日益增长的iOS 14主屏定制趋势，因为它的应用程序可以让你定制图标和小工具背景，甚至允许在小工具内使用小图标或删除图标标签。(请参见上文)。小部件的背景可以设置为与现有墙纸相匹配，也可以使用图像进行配置。
为了更好地利用空间，小部件也可以堆叠在一起，它们包含的图标可以根据一周中的某一天、一天中的某个时间或您所在的位置而变化。这样一来，你就可以拥有一个只在你去健身房时才会出现的小工具，或者一个在办公室里才会出现的小工具。您的主屏幕小部件在工作日也可能与周末不同。
该应用程序的许多功能以前在经典的Today View小部件中提供，但主屏幕小部件的工作方式有所不同。以前，您只能根据时间或位置显示或隐藏固定数量的小部件，而新的小部件支持在不同时间显示的不同图标集。但是，您不能将主屏幕小工具配置为自动消失，因为这会导致主屏幕本身重新排列。
Launcher的创建者格雷格·加德纳(Greg Gardner)表示，由于iOS14的发布，甚至在其更新之前，人们对他的应用程序的兴趣就已经激增，今天发布的iOS14提供了对iOS14小部件的支持。
“iOS14上的人似乎对主屏小工具非常感兴趣，所以他们在App Store上搜索小工具，找到我的应用程序。不幸的是，他们中的一些人对这款应用没有主屏幕小部件感到失望。加德纳补充说：“我希望现在它有了主屏幕部件，下载量会继续增加，新用户不会再失望了。”
IOS14出人意料的发布可能在这方面没有帮助。苹果今年向开发者发出iOS14到来的通知不到24小时，甚至在必要的开发工具(如Xcode)可供下载之前就宣布了这一消息。这意味着一些开发者的iOS14兼容的应用程序在iOS发布当天还没有准备好，就像过去几年一样。
除了扩展的功能之外，Launcher的新应用还有另一个值得欣赏的原因：它的商业模式。
这款应用程序不会通过订阅的方式赚钱，而是只向用户收取扩展功能集的一次性费用。虽然这款应用的早期版本提供了两个不同的定价级别，但Launcher 5已经将定价简化为只有一个。
其新的“高级”应用程序内购买将解锁所有新的主屏幕小部件和定制选项。然而，现有用户只需支付2.99美元的升级费用，而新用户将支付7.99美元的全价。
加德纳说：“实现新的小工具所需的工作量已经足够了，我认为这证明了在应用程序内购买新的东西是合理的。”此外，他指出，App Store也不提供任何官方方式来对这样的场景进行收费升级定价。
Launcher 5现在正在App Store上推出。(如果您没有看到更新日期为9月1日的应用程序。2020年21日，请稍后重试，因为更新可能尚未到达您所在的地区。)
note_en:
The app, which first debuted in 2014, had been well-known for being one of the few to push the envelope when it came to the functionality offered by Apple’s classic Today View widgets. In the years since, the app served as the launchpad for common tasks — like messaging a favorite contact, calling home, getting directions, playing your favorite music and much more right from the Today View.
 Now you can do the same from your home screen, but with a more customizable experience that matches your current iOS 14 theme.
    The app had  once been banned from the App Store for several months because its sole reason for existence was to be a Today View widget provider, without offering other functionality beyond widget configuration. Apple  eventually decided that Launcher had value, despite this limitation, and allowed it back in.
 What’s more, Apple later realized there was a market for workflow automation and  eventually acquired a Launcher competitor of sorts, Workflow, which was turned into Apple’s Shortcuts app and expanded to include additional functionality — like Siri integration, for instance.
 Now, with the release of iOS 14, Apple has fully embraced the idea of customizable widgets for the home screen. Meanwhile, users are leveraging Shortcuts to create custom icons, then building creative home screen themes using a custom combination of widgets, icons and wallpapers.
 Launcher, however, offers a simpler alternative for those who don’t want to spend hours creating a customized experience.
 Instead, you can create a widget with favorite apps and tasks, change the widget’s background color and adjust the icons’ size in one go.
 For example, you can now create Launcher widgets that let you tap an icon to immediately call, message, FaceTime or email a favorite contact; get directions to a location; start playing an artist, album or playlist in Apple Music; access a favorite website; launch actions within apps (like Compose Tweet or Run Shortcut); turn common phone settings on or off (like WiFi, Bluetooth, Low Power Mode, DND, Airplane mode, etc.); or launch any other app on your device.
   Launcher aims to tap into the growing iOS 14 home screen customization trend as its app lets you customize the icons and widget background, even allowing for tiny icons within the widget or removing icon labels. (See above). The widget’s background can be styled to match the existing wallpaper or can be configured using images.
 Widgets can also be stacked for better space utilization and the icons they contain can change based on the day of the week, time of day or your location. That way, you could have a widget that shows up only when you hit the gym, for example, or one that appears when you’re in the office. Your home screen widget could also be different during the work week than on the weekend.
  Many of the app’s features were previously offered in the classic Today View widgets, but the home screen widgets work differently. Where before, you were limited to a fixed number of widgets that could be shown or hidden based on time or location, the new widgets support different icon sets that appear at different times. However, you can’t configure a home screen widget to automatically disappear as that would cause the home screen itself to rearrange.
 Launcher’s creator, Greg Gardner, says he’s seen a surge of interest in his app due to the iOS 14 release, even before its update, out today, which delivers the iOS 14 widget support.
  “People on iOS 14 seem to be pretty excited about home screen widgets, so they are searching the App Store for widgets and are finding my app. Unfortunately some of them have been disappointed that the app didn’t have home screen widgets,” he says. “I hope that now that it has home screen widgets the downloads will continue to increase and the new users won’t be disappointed any longer,” Gardner adds.
 The  surprise release of iOS 14 probably didn’t help things on this front. Apple gave its developers less than 24 hours notice of iOS 14’s arrival this year, even making its announcement before the necessary developer tools (e.g., Xcode) were available for download. That means some developers’ iOS 14-compatible apps weren’t ready and available on the iOS launch day, as in years past.
 In addition to the expanded functionality, there’s another reason to appreciate Launcher’s new app: Its business model.
 The app doesn’t monetize by way of subscriptions but instead only charges its users a one-time fee for an expanded feature set. While the earlier version of the app had offered two different pricing tiers, Launcher 5 has simplified pricing to just the one.
  Its new “Premium” in-app purchase will unlock all the new home screen widgets and customization options. However, existing users only have to pay $2.99 for the upgrade while new users will pay the full price of $7.99.
 “The amount of work required to implement the new widgets was enough that I thought it justified a new in-app purchase,” says Gardner. Plus, he notes, the App Store also doesn’t offer any official means of charging upgrade pricing for scenarios like this.
 Launcher 5 is rolling out  now on the App Store. (If you don’t see the app with an updated date of Sept. 21, 2020, just try again later as the update may not have reached your region yet.)
title: Launcher将其强大的小部件制作应用程序带到iOS 14
title_en: Launcher brings its powerful widget-making app to iOS 14

note:
在社交媒体上引发所有共享屏幕的一大新功能是主屏小工具，这是iOS14首次支持的。这些应用程序可以是第一方的，也可以是第三方的，并且包含在您从App Store下载的应用程序中。有许多开发者竭力确保他们在iOS发布时或接近发布时已经做好了准备，Sarah已经创建了一个不断增加的最佳列表，供您在不确定从哪里开始的情况下查看。
我个人最喜欢的小工具应用程序之一是Widgetsmith，顾名思义，这款应用几乎完全是为了制作它们而创建的。它允许您选择一系列自定义选项，具有许多方便、有用的功能，包括日历、天气和时钟，并提供最适合您的风格的不同字体选择。我一直致力于在iOS系统中创建干净、单一色调的外观，Widgetsmith是迄今为止我发现的最适合创建看起来无边框的主屏幕显示的产品(前提是你的iOS墙纸是纯色，与应用程序支持的颜色之一相匹配)。(如果你的iOS墙纸是纯色的，与应用程序支持的颜色之一相匹配，那么Widgetsmith就是我目前发现的最好的主屏幕显示方式)。
Widget非常擅长提供您通常不想潜入应用程序检索的一目了然的信息，就在您的主屏幕上您需要它们的地方。有些可以捷径到有用的功能，比如谷歌iOS应用中内置的搜索小工具，但大多数都主要是为了减少你实际花在应用本身上的时间。
虽然Widget是新的，但这种定制推送的另一个重要组成部分并不是--那就是为iOS应用程序创建自定义主屏幕图标的能力。自从苹果几年前在iOS上推出快捷键应用程序以来，这一点就一直存在，但随着iOS14中小工具的引入，围绕主屏幕定制的关注度越来越高，许多人第一次发现了这一功能。
严格地说，在iOS上创建自定义图标实际上并不是这样做的-实际上，你正在做的是创建新的快捷方式来触发应用程序的启动，并使用该书签的自定义图像，然后该图像就会出现在你的主屏幕上。这不是一个理想的解决方案，因为这意味着A)你的“应用程序”上不会有任何通知徽章，B)系统首先会引导你进入苹果的快捷方式应用程序，它会在瞬间打开，然后让你进入你为快捷方式选择的实际应用程序。
苹果显然没有为这个用途设计这个快捷方式功能(打开一个目标应用程序意味着一系列自动操作的开始)，但苹果似乎也从来没有真正对让用户选择他们自己的自定义图标感兴趣，所以这是我们目前所能做的最好的事情。幸运的是，这个过程相对简单。不幸的是，这涉及到很多步骤，所以定制整个主屏幕相当耗时。
有一些很棒的例子表明，有创造力的人能够做到这一点，只要给他们一点时间和一些力所能及的努力。随着越来越多的小工具选项不断上线，我们可能也才刚刚开始看到测试苹果规则下可能的界限的局限性。
note_en:
The big new addition that’s prompting all the shared screens across social media are home screen widgets, which are supported under iOS 14 for the first time. These can be either first- or third-party, and are included with apps you download from the App Store. There are a number of developers who pushed to ensure they were ready at or near the launch of iOS, and  Sarah has created a growing list of some of the best for you to check out if you’re not sure where to start.
  One of my personal favorite widget apps is Widgetsmith, an app that, as its name suggest, was created pretty much entirely for the purpose of making them. It allows you a range of customization options, has a number of handy, useful functions including calendar, weather and clock, and comes with different font choices to best suit your style. I’ve always aimed to create a clean, single tone look with iOS as much as possible, and Widgetsmith is the best I’ve found so far for creating homescreen displays that look like they’re borderless (provided your iOS wallpaper is a solid color that matches one of those the app supports).
 Widgets are great at providing at-a-glance information that you don’t typically want to dive into an app to retrieve, right on your homescreen where you need them. Some can shortcut to useful features, like the search widget built into Google’s iOS app, but most are made primarily to reduce the amount of time you spend actually inside the apps themselves.
  While Widgets are new, another big component of this customization push is not – that’s the ability to create custom homescreen icons for iOS apps. That’s been around ever since Apple introduced its Shortcuts app on iOS a couple of years ago, but many people are discovering the feature for the first time as a result of the increased attention around homescreen customization with the introduction of Widgets in iOS 14.
  Creating custom icons on iOS isn’t actually doing that, strictly speaking – what you’re in fact doing is creating new Shortcuts that trigger the launch of an app, and using a custom image for that bookmark that then lives on your homescreen instead. This is not an ideal solution, because it means that A) you won’t have any notification badges on your ‘apps,’ and B) the system first directs you to Apple’s Shortcuts app, which opens for a split-second before bumping you into the actual app you selected for the shortcut.
 Apple clearly didn’t design this Shortcuts feature for this use (opening a target app is meant to be the start of a string of automated actions), but Apple also hasn’t really ever seemed interested in letting users choose their own custom icons, so it’s the best we can do for now. Luckily, the process is relatively simple. Unluckily, there are a lot of steps involved, so it’s pretty time-consuming to customize your entire homescreen.
   There are some fantastic examples out there of what creative individuals have been able to do with this, given a little time and some elbow grease. With more widget options coming online all the time, we’ve probably only begun to see the limits of testing the boundaries of what’s possible under Apple’s rules, too.

note:
我们都笑了，因为这次旅行的目的是谈论Waymo在开发自动驾驶出租车方面对无障碍的承诺，这些出租车已经在凤凰城投入使用。Waymo正在与凤凰城的盲童基金会(FBC)密切合作，以获得对这一经历的反馈，并与旧金山的盲人灯塔进行咨询。我们很高兴地宣布，Waymo在无障碍方面的工作将在Sight Tech Global上展出，这是一个虚拟活动(12月2日至3日)，重点是与人工智能相关的技术将如何影响未来几年的辅助技术和无障碍。免费参加，注册是开放的。
参加Waymo无障碍会议的有三位关键人物，他们帮助指导Waymo的工作。克莱门特·赖特(Clement Wright)是Waymo的产品经理，负责Waymo的用户体验和无障碍工作。他的重点是确保包括残疾人在内的所有Waymo车手都能在Waymo的全无人驾驶服务中享受安全、舒适和方便的乘车服务。马克·阿什顿(Marc Ashton)是盲童基金会(Foundation For Blind Children)的首席执行官，该基金会位于凤凰城，是全国公认的盲童教育领先者。阿什顿的儿子是盲人，这导致他对这一领域感兴趣，并在2007年担任首席执行官。布莱恩·巴申(Bryan Bashin)是旧金山盲人灯塔(LighTower For The Blind)的首席执行官，该公司为加州和世界各地的盲人提供教育、培训、倡导和社区服务。从大学开始失明，巴申职业生涯的大部分时间都致力于倡导平等，为盲人或视力低下的人提供培训和指导。
Waymo追求一种高度无障碍的自动驾驶之旅并不是一项容易的挑战。赖特说：“今天，网约车和出租车司机除了严格驾驶汽车外，还会履行某些职责。”“他们可能会在接车时摇下车窗，与骑车人交谈，帮助他们找到车。在我们努力制造Waymo Driver的过程中，我们面临的最大挑战之一是确保我们了解车主的所有额外需求，而车内没有人类司机。“。
Waymo团队已经与FBC的成年成员合作，收集关于这款用于召唤Waymo出租车的移动应用的反馈，例如，通过应用程序按喇叭的寻路机制。赖特说：“我们一次又一次地看到，一项旨在帮助特定人群(例如视障人士)的功能实际上对我们其余的骑手基础也非常有帮助。这使我们更广泛地关注包容性设计--着眼于特定骑手的需求，以了解关键挑战，然后构建帮助每个人的解决方案。“。
自动驾驶汽车有可能帮助残疾人，包括130万合法失明的美国人，安全高效地到达他们需要的地方。我们将深入研究Waymo在其产品开发周期中是如何考虑无障碍的，并探索来自盲人和低视力用户以及代表这些群体的合作伙伴组织的反馈在这一过程中所起的关键作用。请于12月2日至3日参加我们的全球视界大会。我们现在就可以获得免费通行证。
note_en:
We all had a chuckle because the point of the excursion was to talk about Waymo’s commitment to accessibility in the development of self-driving taxis, which are already in service in Phoenix. Waymo is working closely with the Foundation for Blind Children (FBC) in Phoenix to get feedback on the experience, and also consulting with The Lighthouse for the Blind in San Francisco.  We are delighted to announce that Waymo’s work on accessibility will be featured at   Sight Tech Global , which is a virtual event (December 2-3) focused on how AI-related technologies will influence assistive technology and accessibility in the years ahead. Attendance is free and   registration is open .
 Joining the Waymo accessibility session are three key figures helping to guide Waymo’s work.   Clement Wright  is the Waymo product manager responsible for Waymo’s  user experience and accessibility efforts. His focus is on ensuring all Waymo riders, including those with disabilities, can enjoy safe, comfortable, and convenient rides in Waymo’s fully driverless service.   Marc Ashton  is CEO, Foundation for Blind Children, which is a Phoenix-based and nationally recognized leader in the education of blind children. Ashton’s son is blind, which led to his interest in the field and in 2007 to the role of CEO.   Bryan Bashin  is the CEO of Lighthouse for the Blind in San Francisco, which offers education, training, advocacy, and community for blind  individuals in California and around the world. Blind since college,  Bashin has dedicated much of his career to advocating for equality, access, training and mentorship for individuals who are blind or low vision.
 Waymo’s quest for a highly accessible, self-driving ride is no easy challenge. “ Today, ride-hailing and taxi drivers fulfill certain duties outside of strictly driving the car,” says Wright. “They may roll down the window at pickup to speak to a rider and help them find the car. One of our largest challenges as we work to build the Waymo Driver is ensuring that we understand all of the rider’s additional needs without a human driver in the car.”
 The Waymo team has worked with adult members of the FBC  to get feedback on the mobile app used to summon a Waymo taxi, for example, by using the way finding mechanism of honking the taxi’s horn through the app.  “ Time and again,” says Wright, “we’ve seen that a feature built to help a specific group of people,  the visually impaired for example, is actually very helpful for the rest of our rider base as well. This has led us to a broader focus on inclusive design––looking at specific rider’s needs to understand key challenges, and then building solutions that help everyone.”
 Autonomous vehicles have the potential to help people with disabilities, including the 1.3 million Americans who are legally blind, get where they need to go safely and efficiently.  We will dive into how  Waymo   accounts for accessibility throughout it’s product development cycle and explore the critical role that feedback, from both blind and low vision users, as well as partner organizations who represent those groups, plays in that process. Join us at  Sight Tech Global on December 2-3 to join the session.   Get your free pass now.
title: 失明时叫一辆自驾出租车。了解Waymo如何在Sight Tech Global回答这一挑战
title_en: Hailing a self-driving taxi when blind. Learn how Waymo answers that challenge at Sight Tech Global

这件事始于博士生科林·马德兰(Colin Madland)在推特上谈论一名黑人教职员工与Zoom的问题。根据马德兰的说法，每当说教员使用虚拟背景时，Zoom都会摘掉他的头。
Zoom的一位发言人告诉TechCrunch：“我们已经直接联系用户调查这个问题。”“我们致力于为所有人提供一个包容各方的平台。”
然而，当在推特上讨论这个问题时，算法偏差的问题变得更加严重，因为推特的移动应用程序默认只在预览中显示白人马德伦德的照片。
Twitter的一位发言人在给TechCrunch的一份声明中说：“我们的团队在发货前确实进行了偏见测试，在我们的测试中没有发现种族或性别偏见的证据。”“但从这些例子可以清楚地看出，我们还有更多的分析要做。我们将继续分享我们学到的知识，我们采取的行动，并将开放我们的分析，以便其他人可以审查和复制。“。
Twitter指出，它的首席设计官丹特利·戴维斯(Dantley Davis)发布了一条推文，他自己做了一些实验。戴维斯认为马德兰的面部毛发影响了结果，所以他摘掉了面部毛发，这名黑人教职员工出现在裁剪后的预告片中。在后来的一条推文中，戴维斯说他“和其他人一样对此感到恼火”。不过，我有能力解决这个问题，我会的。“。
推特还指出，卡内基梅隆大学首席科学家维奈·普拉布(Vinay Prabhu)进行了一项独立分析。在他的实验中，他试图看看“种植偏见是不是真的”。
(结果更新)白黑比：40：52(92张图像)使用的代码：https://t.co/qkd9WpTxbK最终注释：https://t.co/OviLl80Eye(我创建了@Cropping_Bias来运行整个实验。正在等待@Twitter批准开发人员凭据)pic.twitter.com/qN0APvUY5f。
Twitter首席技术官帕拉格·阿格拉瓦尔(Parag Agrawal)在回应实验时表示，解决种植偏见是否真实的问题是“一个非常重要的问题”。简而言之，Twitter有时会突显出黑人，有时不会。但Twitter完全做到了，哪怕只有一次，这就足以让它成为问题所在。
这条推特和帖子切中了工作场所发生的事情的症结所在。边缘化的人指出创伤的结果，大多数群体的人致力于证明偏见不会在50%+1%的情况下出现，就好像49%、30%或20%的情况下没有造成创伤一样。Https://t.co/7GRgJyAiGH。
这也说明了一个更大的问题，那就是不良算法的泛滥。正是这些相同类型的算法导致了对黑人的有偏见的逮捕和监禁。它们也是谷歌用来给黑人照片贴上大猩猩标签的算法，微软的Tay机器人也曾成为白人至上主义者。
note_en:
It started when  PhD student Colin Madland tweeted about a Black faculty member’s issues with Zoom. According to Madland, whenever said faculty member would use a virtual background, Zoom would remove his head.
 “We have reached out directly to the user to investigate this issue,” a Zoom spokesperson told TechCrunch. “We’re committed to providing a platform that is inclusive for all.”
    When discussing that issue on Twitter, however, the problems with algorithmic bias compounded when Twitter’s mobile app defaulted to only showing the image of Madlund, the white guy, in preview.
 “Our team did test for bias before shipping the model and did not find evidence of racial or gender bias in our testing,” a Twitter spokesperson said in a statement to TechCrunch. “But it’s clear from these examples that we’ve got more analysis to do. We’ll continue to share what we learn, what actions we take, and will open source our analysis so others can review and replicate.”
 Twitter pointed to a tweet from its chief design officer, Dantley Davis, who ran some of his own experiments.  Davis posited Madland’s facial hair affected the result, so he removed his facial hair and the Black faculty member appeared in the cropped preview.  In a later tweet, Davis said he’s “as irritated about this as everyone else. However, I’m in a position to fix it and I will.”
 Twitter also pointed to an independent analysis from Vinay Prabhu, chief scientist at Carnegie Mellon. In his experiment, he sought to see if “the cropping bias is real.”
 (Results update)  White-to-Black ratio: 40:52 (92 images) Code used:  https://t.co/qkd9WpTxbK Final annotation:  https://t.co/OviLl80Eye (I've created  @cropping_bias to run the complete the experiment. Waiting for  @Twitter to approve Dev credentials)  pic.twitter.com/qN0APvUY5f
 In response to the experiment,  Twitter CTO Parag Agrawal said addressing the question of whether or not cropping bias is real is “a very important question.” In short, sometimes Twitter does crop out Black people and sometimes it doesn’t. But the fact that Twitter does it at all, even once, is enough for it to be problematic.
 This tweet and thread get to the crux of what happens in work places. Marginalized folks point out traumatizing outcomes, majority group folks dedicate themselves to proving bias does not show up in 50+1% of the occasions as if 49% or 30% or 20% of the time doesn’t cause trauma.  https://t.co/7GRgJyAiGH
 It also speaks to the bigger issue of the prevalence of bad algorithms. These same types of algorithms are what leads to biased arrests and imprisonment of Black people. They’re also the same kind of algorithms that Google used to  label photos of Black people as gorillas and that  Microsoft’s Tay bot used to become a white supremacist.
title: Twitter和Zoom的算法偏差问题
title_en: Twitter and Zoom’s algorithmic bias issues

GNOME3桌面环境于2011年正式发布，2020年我们还在3.x版本。是的，尽管在过去(几乎)十年里发生了许多巨大的变化，但我们仍然停留在GNOME 3的点版本上。例如，就在昨天，GNOME 3.38发布了。从历史上看，稳定版本都以偶数结尾，发布前的版本以奇数结尾。对于DE的粉丝们，比如您，我们已经学会了接受这个奇怪的版本控制方案。
好了，各位，对于下一个版本的GNOME，开发人员最终决定从版本3.x开始。您可能认为新版本将是4.0，但您可能大错特错了。实际上，紧随GNOME 3.38之后的是GNOME 40。等等，什么？是的，开发人员实际上正在从3.x迁移到40.x！他们甚至抛弃了偶数/奇数方面，因为40年后的下一个主要稳定版本将是41。较小的稳定更新将被指定为增量点(.1、.2、.3等)。在开发过程中，只会有alpha、beta和发布候选版本--简单明了。可以理解，这会让一些习惯于旧版本控制方案的Linux用户感到困惑。
在发布了近10年的3.x版本之后，次要版本号开始变得笨拙起来。同样非常清楚的是，我们不会因为核心平台的技术变化而改变主要版本，就像我们对GNOME 2和GNOME 3所做的那样，然后在此基础上增加一个重大的UX改变。GNOME基金会的Emmanuele Bassi说，激进的技术和设计更改对维护人员、用户和开发人员来说太具破坏性了；我们已经变得非常善于迭代设计和技术，以至于当前的GNOME平台、UI和UX与GNOME 3.0发布的有很大不同，同时仍然遵循相同的设计原则，#34；GNOME基金会的Emmanuele Bassi说。
不过，为什么要选择40版呢？巴西解释说，因为下一个版本将是3.40，而且这是一个不错的整数。3.38版本是GNOME的第40个版本，但是这个讨论来得太晚，无法有效地切换，所以我们可以说，如果从零开始计算，下一个周期将是GNOME的第40个版本。通过使用40作为基数，我们承认了之前发生的事情，而且我们不会在数字级数中引入很大的不连续，这在某种程度上就是这种变化的意义所在。
正如伟大的威廉·莎士比亚曾经写过的那样，任何名字的玫瑰花都会散发出同样的芳香。就我而言，不管侏儒叫什么，只要它继续很棒就行。话虽如此，我实际上还是喜欢新的版本化方案，因为对于开发人员和新手用户来说，它应该更容易理解。
在惊慌失措或心烦意乱之前，我敦促所有的侏儒粉丝在这里阅读完整的解释。记住，改变有时是好的。
note_en:
The GNOME 3 desktop environment was officially released in 2011, and in 2020 we are still on version 3.x. Yeah, despite many massive changes over the last (almost) decade, we have been stuck with point releases for GNOME 3. For instance, just yesterday,  GNOME 3.38 was released. Historically, the stable releases all ended in even numbers, with pre-release versions ending odd. For fans of the DE, such as yours truly, we have simply learned to live with this odd versioning scheme.
 Well, folks, with the next version of GNOME, the developers have finally decided to move on from version 3.x. You are probably thinking the new version will be 4.0, but you'd be very wrong. Actually, following GNOME 3.38 will be GNOME 40. Wait, what? Yes, the developers are actually moving from 3.x to 40.x! They are even ditching the even/odd aspect, as the next major stable version to come after 40 will be 41. Minor stable updates will be given incremental point designations (.1, .2, .3, etc.). During development, there will just be alpha, beta, and release candidates -- nice and simple. Understandably, this is going to be confusing for some Linux users that are used to the old versioning scheme.
    "After nearly 10 years of 3.x releases, the minor version number is getting unwieldy. It is also exceedingly clear that we’re not going to bump the major version because of technological changes in the core platform, like we did for GNOME 2 and 3, and then piling on a major UX change on top of that. Radical technological and design changes are too disruptive for maintainers, users, and developers; we have become pretty good at iterating design and technologies, to the point that the current GNOME platform, UI, and UX are fairly different from what was released with GNOME 3.0, while still following the same design tenets," says Emmanuele Bassi, The GNOME Foundation.
 Why choose version 40, though? Bassi explains, "Because the next version would be 3.40, and it's a nice, round number. The 3.38 release was the 40th release of GNOME, but this discussion came too late in the cycle to effectively switch, so we can say that, if you start counting at zero, the next cycle would be the 40th release of GNOME. By using 40 as the base, we acknowledge what came before, and we don't introduce a large discontinuity in the number progression, which is somewhat the point of this change."
 As the great William Shakespeare once penned, "A rose by any other name would smell as sweet." As far as I am concerned, it really doesn't matter what GNOME is called, as long as it continues to be excellent. With that said, I actually like the new versioning scheme, as it should be easier to understand for both developers and users that are new to it.
 Before freaking out or getting upset, I urge all GNOME fans to read the full explanation  here. And remember, change  can be good sometimes.
title: GNOME获得新的版本控制方案
